tldr: Avalara is global multi-jurisdiction tax compliance; Freee is a Japan-focused cloud accounting + invoicing platform — only migrate if your business is consolidating into Japan-only operations.
why_switch: Operations are now Japan-centric and global tax engine is overkill
Need integrated JP accounting, payroll, and qualified-invoice issuance, not just tax calc
Want native JP consumption tax and e-invoice (JP PINT) compliance
Prefer a single Japanese vendor over a US-based global suite
Local SMB tooling and ecosystem matter more than 1200+ ERP integrations
what_to_export: Tax codes and product taxability mappings
Historical transactions / invoices and tax line items
export_method: Use Avalara's admin UI to export transactions, customers, exemption certificates, and filing history (CSV/Excel) and pull configuration via the Avalara API where supported. For anything non-obvious, follow Avalara's official export documentation and request a support-assisted export of historical filings to keep an audit trail.
import_method: Freee does not provide a direct Avalara importer. Plan to map Avalara taxability data to Freee's JP tax categories and import customers/invoices via Freee's REST API or CSV upload features. Because Freee is JP-specific, only the Japan-relevant subset of your Avalara data should move; consult Freee's developer documentation for current endpoints and field requirements.

concept_mapping.note: Only JP nexus is meaningful in Freee.

concept_mapping.target: JP consumption tax category in Freee

concept_mapping.note: Carry over JP corporate numbers; foreign VAT IDs may not map.
Re-create historical invoices only if needed for JP audit.
Different model — review JP invoice retention rules.
Endpoints, auth, and rate limits differ; rewrite integration.

gotchas: Freee is Japan-only — non-JP entities cannot be served by it
Tax codes and taxability rules are not 1:1; expect manual mapping
Freee API rate limits require careful batching, especially for backfills
Some Freee documentation is primarily in Japanese
Keep Avalara active for any non-JP filings until fully out
Confirm e-invoice / qualified invoice obligations with a JP CPA

email.subject: Migration plan: Avalara to Freee for JP operations
email.body:
Hi team,

Given our consolidation into Japan, we're planning a move from Avalara to Freee. Freee gives us native JP consumption tax, qualified-invoice issuance, and integrated accounting/payroll in one stack.

We'll export transactions, customers, exemption data, and filing history from Avalara, then map JP-relevant records into Freee via its REST API and CSV import. Tax codes will need manual remapping into Freee's JP categories, and we'll keep Avalara live for any residual non-JP obligations during the transition.

Realistic effort is 2-6 weeks including data mapping, API rewrite, and CPA review. I'll share a detailed cutover plan once we've validated Freee's API limits.

Thanks
